texto: Ida was a natural historian who knew how to throw in enough fiction to keep up dramtic tension. And she was replete with details, like a big fat colorful nineteenth-century historical novel, inching forward slowly....Ida's narrative line, like her waistline, was ample.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marissa Piesman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “Ida was a natural historian who knew how to throw in enough fiction to keep up dramtic tension. A…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, anecdotes, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de humor e vida.

frase_original: Ida was a natural historian who knew how to throw in enough fiction to keep up dramtic tension. And she was replete with details, like a big fat colorful nineteenth-century historical novel, inching forward slowly....Ida's narrative line, like her waistline, was ample.

texto: It often struck Nina that women who seemed capable of only limited mental tasks in most situations could retain information regarding other peoples weight for years, sometimes decades. Unlike genuine idiots savants, they couldn't tell you that December 13, 1972 had fallen on a Wednesday. But they could often tell you, within a pound or two, how much you'd weighed that week.
explicacao: Citação de Marissa Piesman sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “It often struck Nina that women who seemed capable of only limited mental tasks in most situation…” articula humor, vida, women em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como vida e women.
